description_long: This is a classic claw hammer featuring a robust, dark metallic head and a straight, medium-brown wooden handle. The head has a flat striking face on one side and a curved claw on the opposite side, designed for pulling nails. The handle appears to be made of solid wood, showing a natural grain and a slightly weathered texture, suggesting frequent use. The metal head is securely fitted onto the handle, with visible wear and some minor discoloration, indicating age and practical application. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible branding or decorative elements. The hammer is typical of those found in urban garages, workshops, or construction sites.
description: A traditional claw hammer with a dark metal head and a straight wooden handle, showing signs of use and wear. The head features a flat striking face and a curved claw for nail removal.
description_view_0: The hammer is viewed from the top, showing the narrow width of the head and the alignment of the handle.
description_view_1: A side view reveals the full profile of the hammer, highlighting the curved claw and the flat striking face, as well as the straight wooden handle.
description_view_2: The hammer is seen from the bottom, displaying the end of the handle and the underside of the head, with the claw's curvature visible.
description_view_3: Another side view, opposite to view 1, again showing the head's features and the handle's length and grain.
attribute_handle_type: wooden straight handle with visible grain, slightly weathered and smooth, no additional grip enhancements
attribute_head_type: claw hammer head with a flat striking face and a curved claw for nail removal, made of dark steel
attribute_wear_or_damage_marks: visible wear on the handle and minor discoloration on the metal head, but no major damage
